channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Upgrade the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway server software - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand how Microsoft Tunnel Gateway upgrades to new versions of the tunnel software for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Explore guides, tutorials, and references for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: What's new in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find out what's new in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Android device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android and Android Enterprise corporate-owned work profile, personally owned devices with a work profile, fully managed, AOSP, and dedicated devices in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Configure Platform SSO for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to configure Platform SSO and deploy the configuration to your macOS devices. Platform SSO enables single sign-on (SSO) using Microsoft Entra ID with the Secure Enclave, smart card, or password authentication methods. You create a settings catalog policy to configure the settings. This article is a step-by-step guide to configure Platform SSO for macOS devices using Intune.

item.title: Use Multi Admin Approval in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure Multi Admin Approval to protect your tenant against the use of compromised administrative accounts in Intune.

item.title: Device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Android, Android Enterprise, iOS, iPadOS, Linux, macOS, and Windows devices in Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: What is Microsoft Intune? - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune is a cloud-based endpoint management service that secures and manages devices and apps. Learn what it does and how it works.

item.title: Microsoft Cloud PKI for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: An overview of the Microsoft Cloud PKI service, available with Microsoft Intune Suite or as a standalone capability.

item.title: Use Remote Help to Assist Users Authenticated by your Organization - Microsoft Intune
item.description: With the Remote Help app, provide remote assistance to authenticated users who also run the Remote Help app.

item.title: Enable MDM automatic enrollment for Windows - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enable Intune automatic enrollment for Windows devices joining or registering with your Microsoft Entra ID.

item.title: Configure Update Policies for Apple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure software update policies for Apple devices using Apple's Declarative Device Management (DDM) model. Improve security, reduce user disruption, and ensure compliance with reliable, automated updates across your organization.

item.title: App Protection Policies Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Intune app protection policies help protect your company data and prevent data loss.

item.title: Windows device enrollment guide for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Enroll Windows devices using Automatic enrollment, Windows Autopilot, group policy, and co-management enrollment options in Microsoft Intune. Decide which enrollment method to use, and get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks to enroll devices.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Enterprise Application Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Enterprise App Management and the Enterprise App Catalog in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Advanced Analytics Overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover what Microsoft Intune Advanced Analytics is, how it extends endpoint analytics with advanced device insights, proactive troubleshooting, and enhanced reporting.

item.title: Learn about using Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To enhance the security of your organization, set your users to run with standard permissions while Endpoint Privilege Management ensures those users can seamlessly run specified files with elevated rights.

item.title: Security Copilot in Intune features overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Security Copilot in Intune is an AI platform. It can help you create policies, get information about existing policies, and show more details on specific settings, including their impacts on users and devices. You can also use Copilot to troubleshoot device issues and query devices.

item.title: Deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow the steps to deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Role-based access control (RBAC) with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how RBAC lets you control who can perform actions and make changes in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Endpoint analytics overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Discover how Microsoft Intune endpoint analytics provides actionable insights to optimize device performance, improve user experience, and enable proactive IT troubleshooting.

item.title: Set the mobile device management authority - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to set the mobile device management authority in Microsoft Intune and some key considerations.

item.title: Manage Windows Update Ring Policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about Windows Update ring policies for Windows devices, how to create and manage them, and improve update deployment.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Device Query - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device query in Microsoft Intune to get on-demand device state, run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, and troubleshoot devices.

item.title: Configure Universal Print Policy Using Settings Catalog - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure a Universal Print policy using the settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to automatically install printers on managed Windows devices.

item.title: Set up Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 1 for deploying or setting up Intune. The starting point is to review supported configurations, sign up for the trial, configure the custom domain name, add users and groups to Intune, assign licenses to users, manage roles, grant admin permissions, and set the MDM authority.

item.title: Sign Up for Microsoft Intune Free Trial Setup Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sign up for a Microsoft Intune free trial and configure your tenant. Learn the setup process, prerequisites, and how to configure domain names. Start evaluating Intune today.

item.title: Assign Apps to Groups in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to assign an Intune app to groups of users or devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Update Microsoft 365 Apps With the Settings Catalog in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use settings catalog in Microsoft Intune to update Microsoft 365 apps to the latest version, and choose how frequently Office checks for updates. See the device registry keys that are updated when an Intune policy to Office update is applied.

item.title: Migration guide to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deployment guide to set up, onboard, or move to Intune. These steps include moving from partner MDM providers, using co-management, moving from on-premises group policy, and moving from Office 365 device management.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Get started with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See an overview of the steps to start using Intune. Plan your move and deployment of Intune, determine your licensing needs and any platform requirements, use compliance and Conditional Access, deploy apps, create device configuration profiles, and enroll your devices to be managed. Get more information on mobile application management for BYOD or personal devices.

item.title: Prerequisites the Microsoft Tunnel VPN for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Review the prerequisites for installation and use of the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. These include Linux servers, network, and firewall configurations.

item.title: Tutorial - Walkthrough the Microsoft Intune Admin Center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you will tour Microsoft Intune in the Microsoft Intune admin center to better understand how to accomplish tasks.

item.title: Using Remote Help on Windows to Assist Authenticated Users - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Remote Help app to provide remote assistance to authenticated users who also run the Remote Help app, and to troubleshoot for frontline workers (FLW).

item.title: Deployment guide to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Get started with macOS endpoints - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune guide to set up and configure macOS devices from setup to creating policies and enrolling devices. You secure your corporate and organization-owned endpoints that are enrolled in Intune, and then deploy at scale with Apple Business Manager or Apple School Manager.

item.title: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune for Device Compliance - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Integrate Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Microsoft Intune as a Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) solution to enforce device compliance and prevent security breaches.

item.title: Deploy endpoint detection and response policy with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune endpoint security policies to configure and deploy Microsoft Defender for Endpoint EDR capabilities to Windows, macOS, and Linux devices.

item.title: Learn about the Microsoft Tunnel VPN solution for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway, a VPN server for Intune that runs on Linux. With Microsoft Tunnel, cloud-based devices you manage with Intune can reach your on-premises infrastructure.

item.title: Add groups to organize users and devices for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Microsoft Entra groups to organize users and devices for use with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: A guide to managing iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Plan and Prepare for Endpoint Privilege Management Deployment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan your Endpoint Privilege Management deploying by understanding requirements, fundamentals, and security recommendations.

item.title: Add an Enterprise App Catalog App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add an Enterprise App Catalog app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Creating elevation rules with Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to create strong file elevation rules with Microsoft Intune Endpoint Privilege Management

item.title: ServiceNow integration with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: ServiceNow integration enables help desk staff, who are licensed to use Remote Help, to use ServiceNow to view incidents and details of the tech issue that an employee is facing from the Troubleshooting pane. This article also describes how to configure ServiceNow integration with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure security baseline policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that establish a default and recommended security postures on Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Get Started With the Microsoft Intune App SDK - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Quickly enable your mobile app for mobile application management (MAM) with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use the latest TeamViewer integration in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the latest TeamViewer integration in Microsoft Intune to initiate remote assistance sessions for managed devices.

item.title: App Protection Policy Settings for Windows - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes the app protection policy (APP) settings for MAM on Windows.

item.title: Use direct enrollment for macOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy and enroll macOS devices in Microsoft Intune using direct enrollment with Apple Configurator.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned userless devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned userless devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Learn about using Microsoft Tunnel with Mobile Application Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Tunnel for MAM with Android and iOS devices. Tunnel for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that aren't or can't enroll with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune architecture - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Reference architecture for a Microsoft Intune deployment, including cloud and on-premises components and Microsoft and third-party integrations.

item.title: Resource Performance Report - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the resource performance report in Intune to analyze CPU and RAM trends, identify at-risk devices, and act on recommendations to improve user experience.

item.title: Vulnerability Remediation Agent Overview and Set Up - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Vulnerability Remediation Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to set it up. The agent uses AI to identify and prioritize vulnerability remediation based on Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management data.

item.title: Managing Elevation Settings for Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to manage the Endpoint Privilege Management client, including reporting level and default elevation response.

item.title: Frequently asked questions about MAM and app protection - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides answers to some frequently asked questions on Intune mobile application management (MAM) and Intune app protection.

item.title: Zebra LifeGuard Over-the-Air Integration with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage firmware updates for supported Zebra devices.

item.title: Set up Android (AOSP) device management in Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Set up Intune for corporate-owned user-associated devices built on the Android Open Source Project (AOSP) platform.

item.title: Sign up or sign in to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: How to sign up for a Microsoft Intune subscription or sign in to start with your subscription.

item.title: Add, Configure, and Protect Apps with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, and protect apps with Intune.

item.title: Use EPM support approvals for file elevation requests with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage support approvals for elevation requests when you use Endpoint Privilege Management for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Government Service overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Intune government service offerings and features. This article is designed to serve as an overview of the Microsoft Intune offering for government community cloud (GCC) High and United States Department of Defense (DoD) environments.

item.title: Manage Android FOTA updates with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune to manage firmware updates on Android devices. A FOTA update can include software and security patches, feature updates, and other changes to the device's firmware.

item.title: Assign a role to an Intune administrator - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to assign a built-in or custom role to a user in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Settings you can manage with Intune Endpoint Protection profiles for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the available settings in Intune endpoint protection profiles for managed Windows devices.

item.title: Deploy Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand the steps and phases for deploying Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide for Linux device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Linux device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Turn on iOS/iPadOS supervised mode with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to turn on iOS/iPadOS supervised mode with Intune.

item.title: Endpoint security in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to manage endpoint security in Microsoft Intune. Configure security policies, deploy baselines, and integrate with Defender for Endpoint to protect devices.

item.title: Battery Health Report in Endpoint Analytics - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use the battery health report in Microsoft Intune to detect battery degradation, plan proactive replacements, and improve user experience.

item.title: Overview of Apple Automated Device Enrollment for iOS/iPadOS in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about automated device enrollment (ADE) for Apple mobile devices in Microsoft Intune, including supported scenarios, key features, and how to get started.

item.title: Learn about Policy Configuration Agent and set it up - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Policy Configuration Agent in Microsoft Intune, its prerequisites, how it works, and how to set it up. The agent is a generative AI feature that helps create Intune policies based on uploaded documents or benchmarks. The Policy Configuration Agent is a feature of Security Copilot in Intune.

item.title: Anomalies Report for Proactive Device Issue Detection - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the anomalies report in Advanced Analytics to monitor device health, identify issues early, and prioritize fixes to improve endpoint reliability.

item.title: How to use Microsoft Intune documentation - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to search, provide feedback about, and contribute to Microsoft Intune product family documentation. This documentation also covers Configuration Manager, Intune, Endpoint analytics, and Windows Autopilot.

item.title: Zero Trust with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune contributes to a strong Zero Trust strategy and architecture by managing and securing endpoints that access organizational resources.

item.title: Device Timeline Report - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device timeline report in Intune to review device event history, identify issues faster, and correlate incidents to start troubleshooting now.

item.title: Add users to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add and managing Microsoft Entra user accounts from within Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Manage Specialty devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides information about specialty devices and how can you manage them with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Troubleshoot and monitor Remote Help for Microsoft Intune. - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the troubleshooting and monitoring information for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune to ensure healthy and successful deployment.

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for iOS Developer Guide - Plan the Integration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: The Microsoft Intune App SDK for iOS lets you incorporate Intune app protection policies (also known as MAM policies) into your native iOS app. Plan the integration.

item.title: Known Issues for Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure policies that define how Endpoint Privilege Management functions in your tenant, and behaviors when elevating files to run in administrative context.

item.title: Protection and configuration levels overview in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the different levels of protection and configuration in Microsoft Intune, including minimum, enhanced, and high levels. Understand the recommended policies for apps, compliance, and device configuration at each level to secure your organization's resources effectively.

item.title: Deployment guide for Windows device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Windows device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Endpoint Privilege Management to transition users from administrator to standard user - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand the steps and phases for using Endpoint Privilege Management to transition users from administrators to standard users.

item.title: Manage Apple mobile devices and tokens in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sync devices, distribute devices, and re-enroll devices for Apple automated device enrollment on iOS/iPadOS in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune App SDK for Android Developer Integration and Testing Guide - Plan the Integration - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan the integration to incorporate Intune mobile app management (MAM) into your Android app.

item.title: Understand App Protection Policy Delivery and Timing - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the different deployment windows for app protection policies to understand when changes should appear on your end user devices.

item.title: Configure security, email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 4 to deploy device configuration profiles as part of the minimum set of policies for your devices using Microsoft Intune. The starting point is to enable the firewall, install AV, scan for malware, install software updates, create a strong PIN policy, and create email, VPN, and Wi-Fi device configuration profiles.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Intune for increased security - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to improve your security posture with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use Microsoft Tunnel VPN with iOS/iPad devices that don't enroll with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) for iOS to the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. Tunnel support for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that can't or haven't enrolled with Microsoft Intune. Admins should use this article to create policies that support per-app VPN in line of business (LOB) apps.

item.title: Device Scopes - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device scopes in Microsoft Intune with scope tags for custom device reporting and targeted insights.

item.title: Configure applications with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to configure applications with Microsoft Intune in preparation for device deployment.

item.title: Microsoft Intune servicing information and details - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the frequency of the Microsoft Intune service updates, the release cadence, and how to check your tenant release version.

item.title: Use Microsoft Tunnel VPN with Android devices that don't enroll with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add support for Mobile Application Management (MAM) for Android to the Microsoft Tunnel Gateway. Tunnel support for MAM expands access to your organizational resources for devices that can't or haven't enrolled with Microsoft Intune
